data.title: MAHA report under attack for alleged fake sources, misinterpretations
data.description:
A report issued a week ago by the Make America Healthy Again Commission that heavily attacked overmedicalization, poor diet, and vaccine schedules as factors behind childhood diseases is now facing its own scrutiny amid accusations that some citations included in the assessment are made up. 

The MAHA report contains references to more than 500 studies and other sources used to back up arguments. However, seven of those sources don't exist, according to reporting from NOTUS. 

One such study dealing with anxiety and adolescents included was published in JAMA Pediatrics listing Katherine Keyes as the first author.  NOTUS reached Keyes who told the online publication that she wasn't involved with that article.

"The paper cited is not a real paper that I or my colleagues were involved with," Keyes said. "We've certainly done research on this topic, but did not publish a paper in JAMA Pediatrics on this topic with that co-author group, or with that title."

NOTUS added that a link to the article in the report is broken and although the citation lists the edition it appeared in, a look at that edition doesn't show the article in it.

One part of the report discusses "corporate capture of media" and provides citations for two studies it claims provides evidence that direct-to-consumer ads has led to a rise in children's prescriptions for ADHD and depression. 

Those citations lead to table of content pages for editions of their respective journals where those studies don't exist.

NOTUS also faulted the MAHA report for how it interpreted certain studies. For example, the assessment cites a review paper that claims that therapy alone is as or more effective than drugs. But one of the statisticians who worked on the paper told NOTUS that conclusion is wrong because their research didn't try to compare therapy’s effectiveness to medications.

data.title: Rates can come down if inflation resumes trend down, labor stays strong, Chicago Fed's Goolsbee says
data.description:
If inflation trends down, as it was before the April 2 tariffs, and the economy remains at full employment, then interest rates can come down, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee said on Thursday at the Mackinac Policy Conference.

"If you can get the dust out of the air, I believe what's under there is a strong dual-mandate economy," he said. The dual mandate is the Federal Reserve's charge from Congress to preserve price stability and maximum employment.

As it stands now, traders price in a 97.9% chance that the Federal Open Market Committee will keep the federal funds rate target range at 4.25%-4.50% at its June 17-18 meeting, according to the CME FedWatch tool. For the September meeting, the probability of a 25-basis point cut rises to 51.2%, up from a 9.4% probability a month ago.

"Tariffs, for sure, are much more impactful on the Midwest," especially the Chicago Fed's district, which skews more toward agriculture and manufacturing than the nation as a whole, Goolsbee said.

"Imported goods are only 11% of the U.S. economy," he said. But there are three clear ways tariffs can "jump out of their lane" — retaliatory tariffs, if they bleed into domestic manufacturing, or if people change their behavior. 

That could pose the potential for inflation to resurge as the economy stagnates, what's often called "stagflation."

"Stagflation is the toughest scenario for a central bank, because there's no playbook," Goolsbee said. That can put the two sides of the mandate into conflict. "This is not 1970s-style stagflation."

He also stressed that an independent central bank is critical. "If politics gets to control the interest rate, then inflation is coming back," he said. President Donald Trump has long been voicing his view that the Fed should cut rates to encourage growth. Also, Richard Nixon and Lyndon B. Johnson had pressured the central bank to lower rates.

Paul Volcker, the Fed chair from 1979 to 1987, had jacked up the federal funds rate to 20% to break inflation that had surged in the late 1970s and early '80s, he pointed out. 

"COVID was by far the weirdest recession we ever had," Goolsbee said. That was followed by the "weirdest recovery we've ever had."

Housing inflation has been persistent, though it has started to come down. "If you look at the relative price of housing, the price of goods vs. the price of housing, it has been an extreme case in the last couple of years," he observed. That has made the relative price of housing, compared with other goods, "a lot more expensive."

data.title: Inspira stock soars on initiating global commercial rollout of ART100 system
data.description:
• Inspira Technologies (NASDAQ:IINN) has announced a strategic shift to accelerate the commercialization of its U.S. FDA cleared INSPIRA™ ART100 system, while maintaining the development and regulatory pathways of its portfolio of products.
  • This development follows the initial clinical implementation of the ART100 system in medical centers in the U.S., where the device has been used to treat patients in real-world clinical settings.
  • Based on the current ongoing discussions, deliveries are expected to begin in the second half of 2025, subject to logistical and regulatory alignment, and the finalization of commercial and legal terms.
  • "We recognize the immediate market potential for our ART100 system. This approach seeks to maximize shareholder value in the near and long term." said Dagi Ben Noon, CEO of Inspira Technologies.
  • IINN is +16.3% to $0.50.

data.title: VivoPower receives revised non-binding proposal from Energi for 51% stake in Tembo at $200M
data.description:
• VivoPower International (NASDAQ:VVPR) has received a revised non-binding proposal from Energi Holdings to acquire a 51% controlling stake in its subsidiary Tembo e-LV B.V.
  • The proposed deal values Tembo at an enterprise value of $200M, with adjustments to the equity purchase price based on net debt and other customary factors.
  • Energi supports Tembo’s planned merger with Cactus Acquisition Corp. 1 (OTC:CCTSF) and may roll its 51% stake into the combined entity post-transaction.
  • VivoPower would retain a significant shareholding in Tembo if the business combination proceeds.
  • Proceeds from the potential sale would be used to optimize VivoPower’s capital structure, including debt repayment and potentially returning surplus funds to shareholders.
  • VVPR shares up 7% on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Boeing to resume deliveries to China next month; CEO aims to ramp up MAX production
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) +3.1% in Thursday's trading after CEO Kelly Ortberg said China is set to resume taking deliveries of the company's aircraft in June, after halting deliveries in April in retaliation to U.S. tariffs.

"China has now indicated... they're going to take deliveries. The first deliveries will be next month," Ortberg said at a Bernstein conference.

Boeing (NYSE:BA) has been paying tariffs on imported components from Italy and Japan for its wide-body Dreamliner planes, which are made in South Carolina, Ortberg said, adding that much of the tariffs can be recouped when the planes are exported again.

The CEO also said he wants to raise production of 737 MAX jets from the current monthly rate in the mid-30s to 47/month by the end of this year, and expects to reach monthly production of its best-selling plane to 42 by mid-year.

Boeing (BA) 737 production currently is capped at 38/month, a limit imposed by the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration last year after a midair accident involving a door plug that was not secured at the factory put the company's safety and production quality program under added scrutiny.

data.title: Royal Caribbean plans a $250M investment to update Solstice series ships
data.description:
Royal Caribbean Cruise Ltd. (NYSE:RCL) plans to make an investment to freshen the classic Solstice Series ships that operate under the Celebrity Cruises brand. The company is investing more than $250 million to modernize the best-in-class ships, starting with Celebrity Solstice in 2026.

The cruise line operator said guests can look forward to eight new Celebrity Cruises experiences, including four new-to-sea spaces and many redesigned favorites. The innovations include reinvented outdoor escapes with enhanced views, new restaurants, and entertainment designed to create moments of connection with loved ones, as well as a redesigned stay with all new stateroom categories,

Celebrity Solstice will be the first of the Solstice Series ships to be made new again, welcoming guests for reimagined vacation experiences starting in March 2026.

"Our Solstice Series ships represent the best of classic cruising, and now we're elevating this vacation experience even further by adding brand new experiences and enhancements at every turn," stated Laura Hodges Bethge, president of Celebrity Cruises.

Miami-based Celebrity Cruises is considered a premium cruise line. Founded in 1988, Celebrity has developed a reputation for offering an upscale cruising experience, focusing on modern design, elevated dining, and attentive service across its fleet of ships. Celebrity Xcel is the highly anticipated fifth vessel in Celebrity Cruises' Edge-class series, scheduled to debut in November 2025.

data.title: Best Buy lowers full-year comparable sales range to -1% to +1% as tariff mitigation strategies evolve
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Best Buy Co., Inc. (BBY) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Corie Barry, CEO, highlighted that "we executed well in Q1 and delivered better than expected profitability," with first quarter revenue of $8.8 billion, an adjusted operating income rate of 3.8%, and adjusted earnings per share of $1.15. She noted that computing, mobile phones, and tablets drove comparable sales growth, but this was offset by declines in home theater, appliances, and drones, resulting in a domestic comparable sales decline of 0.7%.
  • Barry stated, "Our omnichannel operations provided strong support for our Q1 online sales, which grew year-over-year for the second consecutive quarter. They were nearly 32% of total domestic sales, a slightly higher mix than last year."
  • Addressing tariffs, Barry revealed, "We currently estimate the percentage of product COGS [from China] is approximately 30% to 35% compared to the 55% metric we shared in March. This is the result of vendors using production capabilities in multiple countries..."
  • She emphasized, "As of mid-May, we have already made the related price and promotional adjustments to our assortment."
  • Best Buy updated its annual outlook, lowering its full year comparable sales range to down 1% to up 1% and expects an adjusted operating income rate of approximately 4.2%.
  • Barry shared that the company is "on track to launch our innovative improved search experience across dot-com, small view, and our app later this year" and is also preparing for the upcoming Switch 2 launch with unique in-store events.
  • Best Buy Marketplace is set for a mid-year launch, with Barry noting, "We have already exceeded the seller count goal we set for the entire year. We expect to have at least 500 of them onboarded and ready for the initial mid-year launch."
  • CFO Matt Bilunas stated, "Our adjusted operating income rate of 3.8% was approximately 40 basis points better than expected, which was primarily driven by favorable SG&A expense."

Outlook

  • The company lowered its full year comparable sales range to down 1% to up 1% and expects an adjusted operating income rate of approximately 4.2%.
  • Best Buy updated its revenue guidance to a range of $41.1 billion to $41.9 billion and adjusted diluted earnings per share of $6.15 to $6.30.
  • Bilunas stated, "Our guidance also assumes that there is no material change in consumer behavior from the trends we have seen in recent quarters."
  • The company expects its gross profit rate to be slightly unfavorable to last year, driven by a higher portion of sales from lower-margin categories such as computing.

Financial Results

  • Best Buy reported enterprise revenue of $8.8 billion for the quarter, a decrease of 0.9% versus last year.
  • Adjusted operating income rate was flat year-over-year at 3.8%, while adjusted diluted earnings per share decreased 4% to $1.15.
  • Domestic gross profit rate increased 10 basis points to 23.5%, while international gross profit rate decreased 80 basis points to 22%.
  • Domestic adjusted SG&A decreased $13 million, primarily due to a favorable indirect tax settlement.
  • The company incurred $109 million in restructuring charges, mainly related to Best Buy Health.
  • Best Buy returned $302 million to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases during the quarter.

Q&A

  • Scot Ciccarelli, Truist Securities, asked about the significant change in China sourcing. Barry reiterated, "China has come down to 30% to 35% compared to the 55% metric... leveraging manufacturing flexibility... increasing country diversification... negotiating costs... adjusting assortments... and finally... adjusting prices."
  • Michael Baker, DA Davidson, inquired about demand pull-forward and market share. Bilunas explained, "It's a little hard to look at the specific impact to a pull-forward... because of the shifting of the events for Easter." Barry added, "I think in Q1, we maybe saw a little bit of share loss, but we have really good plans for the rest of the year."
  • Greg Melich, Evercore ISI, focused on 3P growth and advertising. Bilunas noted that ad sales currently come through in the margin category, with future growth expected.
  • Loren Eng, Morgan Stanley, questioned the bridge between positive category sentiment and updated comp guidance. Bilunas said, "We applied an updated view of what we believe the tariff impacts could be based on all the commentary that we've already given."
  • Peter Keith, Piper Sandler, confirmed the Marketplace mid-year launch and asked about cannibalization. Barry replied that the initiative remains accretive overall after considering investments and cannibalization.
  • Steven Zaccone, Citi, asked about the drivers to "comp the comp" in the back half of the year. Bilunas cited Windows 10 support ending, AI innovation, and ongoing store experience updates as supportive factors.
  • Brian Nagel, Oppenheimer, pressed on the balance between sales and gross margin under tariffs. Barry responded, "The balance... is navigating this environment in a way that stimulates consumer demand."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed slight concern about tariff exposure, sourcing shifts, and the sustainability of sales and margin performance, often probing for clarity on category trends, mitigation strategies, and guidance assumptions.
  • Management maintained a measured and detailed tone in prepared remarks, with Barry and Bilunas methodically explaining mitigation efforts and scenario planning. Barry emphasized, "We will continue to scenario plan and adjust with agility as the situation evolves."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone was somewhat more cautious given tariff uncertainties, while analysts were more focused on granular impacts and competitive positioning.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from flat to up 2% comparable sales in Q4 2025 to down 1% to up 1% in Q1 2026.
  • Management updated the estimated percentage of COGS from China, indicating a significant reduction from 55% to 30%–35%.
  • Strategic focus on launching the Best Buy Marketplace and driving advertising revenue remains, with more detailed rollout plans and seller onboarding progress this quarter.
  • Analysts’ questions continued to focus on tariffs, market share, and the ramp-up of new profit streams, but there was increased scrutiny on the effectiveness of mitigation strategies.
  • Management’s tone shifted from emphasizing momentum and execution to highlighting adaptability and uncertainty around tariffs.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing uncertainty related to tariff levels, timing, and the potential reaction of American consumers as key risks.
  • Barry noted, "There is still uncertainty related to tariff levels, timing, and countries involved in addition to the potential actions of others in the industry, as well as the potential reaction of American consumers."
  • Mitigation strategies include manufacturing flexibility, cost negotiations, country diversification, assortment adjustments, and price changes.
  • Analysts highlighted concerns about possible demand pull-forward, market share shifts, and the elasticity of consumer demand in light of price increases.

Final Takeaway

Best Buy’s management outlined a solid execution in Q1 2026 despite revenue softness and ongoing tariff headwinds, with strong performance in computing, mobile phones, and tablets partially offsetting other category declines. The company lowered its full-year comparable sales guidance and emphasized robust mitigation strategies against tariff impacts, focusing on manufacturing flexibility, cost negotiations, and supply chain diversification. Management remains committed to driving new profit streams through the launch of Best Buy Marketplace and expanding advertising, while maintaining flexibility to adapt as trade conditions and consumer behavior evolve throughout the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Iradimed rises after FDA nod for infusion pump system
data.description:
• Shares of Iradimed (NASDAQ:IRMD) jumped on Thursday after the MedTech announced the FDA’s 510((k)) clearance for its next MRidium 3870 IV infusion pump system.
  • With the approval, the product becomes the latest addition to the Winter Springs, Florida-based company’s MRI-compatible IV infusion pump systems, the first of which cleared the FDA’s 510((k)) hurdle in 2025.
  • “We are thrilled to receive FDA 510((k)) clearance for the MRidium® 3870, a milestone that underscores our commitment to advancing MRI-compatible medical technology,” CEO Roger Susi said.
  • The company has planned a gradual commercial rollout of the MRidium 3870, with its initial units expected to reach select healthcare facilities in Q4 2025. Thereafter, the commercial shipments are expected to gather steam throughout 2026, Iradimed (NASDAQ:IRMD) said.

data.title: Wolters Kluwer appoints Greg Samios as CEO of Health Division
data.description:
• Wolters Kluwer (OTCPK:WTKWY) has named Greg Samios as CEO of its Health division, effective June 1.
  • Samios succeeds Stacey Caywood, who became a Member of the Executive Board of Wolters Kluwer on May 15, 2025.  
  • In a prior role at Wolters Kluwer, Samios served as Executive Vice President of the Legal & Regulatory U.S. business.
